basics.name: Spencer Kittleson
basics.label: Senior Full Stack Engineer & Technical Lead
basics.summary: Senior full-stack engineer and technical lead with 8+ years of experience building and scaling enterprise SaaS platforms. Deep expertise in .NET migrations, observability (OpenTelemetry), authentication/authorization, and AI/ML integration. Proven track record of 1,650+ commits, 672 merged PRs, and 1,701+ code reviews at MindTouch/NICE. Passionate about developer tooling, IoT/embedded systems, and mentoring teams.

work.name: NICE CXone Expert (formerly MindTouch)
work.position: Senior Full Stack Engineer & Technical Lead
work.summary: Leading platform engineering for NICE CXone Expert, a large-scale SaaS knowledge management platform serving enterprise customers worldwide. Lead .NET migrations, OpenTelemetry observability integration, authentication/authorization systems, and AI/ML developer enablement across a distributed team. Manage and mentor developers while driving architectural decisions across .NET/C# backend, TypeScript/JavaScript frontend, and AWS infrastructure.
highlights: Led multi-year .NET migration from Mono to .NET 8 targeting .NET 10, modernizing the entire stack

Architected and implemented OpenTelemetry observability across the monolith — instrumenting 80% of critical components with distributed tracing, metrics, and structured logging using Grafana and OpenObserve
Led design and development of Unified Knowledge Service (UKS), a federated knowledge platform with multi-tenant SSO and Cognigy integration
Redesigned authentication/authorization enablement — built self-service SSO setup, and consolidated legacy auth patterns
Responded to and documented security incidents including an authentication bypass in the legacy API
Migrated LambdaSharp infrastructure to AWS CDK for improved deployment reliability
Led RDS encryption rollout across production databases
Built developer tooling enforcing local test execution before PR submission
Mentored and managed a distributed developer team of 10-12 engineers across multiple time zones
Built AI-assisted technical documentation pipeline using LLMs for gap analysis and content generation
Created internal reference architectures and architectural decision records (ADRs) for AWS services, microservices, and authentication patterns
Drove developer productivity improvements — reported measurable error reduction from AI code reviewer integration in CI/CD
1,650+ commits, 672 merged PRs, 1,701+ PRs reviewed across 8 years on the MindTouch/Deki monorepo
Built and maintained LambdaSharp Tool (dotnet 8) for AWS Lambda deployment and management

volunteer.name: Home Automation Community
volunteer.summary: Shared expertise in home automation through community presentations and public talks, covering MQTT infrastructure, ESP32/ESP8266 embedded systems, and long-term IoT deployment strategies.
highlights: Delivered multi-part presentation series on home automation, lessons learned, and IoT architecture
Shared technical write-ups on MQTT broker design, ESP32 firmware development, and network-isolated IoT deployments

projects.name: ESP-TypeScript
projects.description: Transpile TypeScript to Espruino JavaScript for ESP8266/ESP32 microcontrollers — enabling safe, maintainable, testable IoT firmware development.
highlights: 164 GitHub stars — most starred personal repository
Bridges TypeScript type safety to bare-metal ESP8266/ESP32 via Espruino transpilation
Enables full TypeScript features (interfaces, generics, async/await) on $5 microcontrollers
Template project for writing production-grade, type-checked embedded firmware

projects.name: bluetooth-wos
projects.description: Bluetooth Wall of Sheep — terminal-based Bluetooth Low Energy hacking and monitoring tool for discovering, scanning, and interacting with BLE devices.
highlights: 15 GitHub stars
Built with Python for BLE scanning, device discovery, and protocol analysis
Terminal-based interface for real-time Bluetooth traffic monitoring
Supports hacking and reverse-engineering of BLE protocols

projects.name: mqtt_broker_esp
projects.description: Standalone MQTT 3.1.1 broker built from scratch in C for ESP32-S3 — designed for 10-year deployment on $10 silicon with no external dependencies, no cloud, no broker library.
highlights: 9 GitHub stars
~2,800 lines of custom C against lwIP BSD sockets handling CONNECT/SUBSCRIBE/PUBLISH/QoS 0+1/retained/wildcards/$SYS
Supports up to 100 concurrent clients on 8MB chip with PSRAM for retained store and in-flight tables
129 pytest assertions running against live ESP32 hardware on every release
Built-in Tasmota-style scheduled publishes, Berry scripting VM, SNTPv4 server, OTA with dual partitions
Featured on Hacker News with detailed technical discussion

projects.name: jarvis
projects.description: Local-first AI voice assistant using Piper TTS, OpenAI Whisper, and Ollama for on-device speech-to-text, LLM reasoning, and text-to-speech.
highlights: 5 GitHub stars
Fully local pipeline: Whisper for STT, Ollama for LLM, Piper for TTS — no cloud dependencies
Demonstrated viable local voice assistant architecture on commodity hardware

projects.name: esp32-watch
projects.description: ESP32-S3 smartwatch firmware with MicroPython/LVGL UI and BLE companion PWA — a full custom watch firmware stack.
highlights: Custom firmware using MicroPython and LVGL for rich graphical UI
BLE companion PWA for configuration and data sync
End-to-end embedded product: hardware, firmware, and web companion

projects.name: Do Code That Matters
projects.description: Personal blog about software development, 3D printing, DIY, dotnet, raspberry pis, and C#.
highlights: Built with Astro, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript
3 GitHub stars

projects.name: GcodeController
projects.description: C# application to manage and control sending G-code to serial CNC/3D printing devices.
highlights: 7 GitHub stars
Built with C# and .NET for serial communication with CNC/3D printers
G-code parsing, queuing, and real-time device control

projects.name: whisper-note
projects.description: CLI dictation tool powered by OpenAI Whisper for fast voice-to-text note-taking.
highlights: 2 GitHub stars
Python CLI leveraging Whisper for real-time speech-to-text
Quick note capture via voice input

projects.name: udp-to-websocket
projects.description: Bridge that forwards UDP server traffic to WebSocket connections for real-time web integration.
highlights: 2 GitHub stars
Enables UDP protocol data to be consumed by web browsers via WebSocket
Useful for integrating legacy UDP systems with modern web frontends
